title: Add --enable-webhooks CLI flag for webhook gating
summary: Adds --enable-webhooks boolean CLI flag (default true) to cmd/main.go that conditionally creates the webhook server and registers admission webhooks. Extracts buildWebhookServer() for testability. Helm deployment template passes --enable-webhooks=false when .Values.webhook.enabled is false. Table-driven Go unit tests and Helm template tests cover all cases.

tests_checklist.item: Go unit tests exist and pass (11/11 in cmd package)
Helm template tests exist and pass (303/303)
Edge cases covered (enabled/disabled with and without TLS opts)

code_quality_checklist.item: Helm conditional follows existing leaderElection.enabled pattern
No dead code or commented-out code

security_checklist.item: No user input validation needed (boolean CLI flag)
No secrets or sensitive data exposed

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — extracted function + conditionals
Nil webhook.Server correctly disables webhook listener in ctrl.Options
Webhook registration skip prevents TLS cert loading
Backward compatible — flag defaults to true

fail_fast_checklist.item: Webhook setup errors still exit immediately when enabled
Info log emitted when webhooks disabled

defensive_checklist.item: Flag has safe default (true = existing behavior)
No external inputs to validate beyond CLI flag

review_steps.1_intent: Add --enable-webhooks CLI flag to prevent CrashLoopBackOff when deployed without cert-manager TLS
review_steps.2_scope: Changes limited to cmd/main.go, cmd/main_test.go, deployment.yaml, deployment_test.yaml, and planwerk tracking JSON — no scope creep
review_steps.3_verification: go test PASS, go vet PASS, golangci-lint 0 issues, helm unittest 303 PASS
review_steps.4_patterns: buildWebhookServer follows parseWatchNamespaces extraction pattern; Helm conditional follows leaderElection.enabled pattern; tests follow table-driven pattern
review_steps.5_tests: 4 Go test cases (enabled/disabled x with/without TLS opts) + 2 Helm test cases (default omits flag, webhook.enabled=false includes flag)
review_steps.6_security: No security concerns — boolean CLI flag controlling internal webhook lifecycle
review_steps.7_complexity: Minimal implementation: one extracted function, two conditional blocks, one log statement
review_steps.8_completeness: All 4 tasks implemented (1.1: flag+conditionals, 1.2: Go tests, 2.1: Helm template, 2.2: Helm tests). All 3 user stories satisfied

detected_patterns.name: Extract flag-dependent logic into testable pure functions
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding new CLI flags to cmd/main.go whose logic produces a value or side effect that can be isolated from the manager lifecycle
detected_patterns.description: Flag-dependent logic that produces values consumed by ctrl.NewManager or subsequent setup calls is extracted into standalone functions (not methods, not closures) at package scope. These functions take the flag value and any dependencies as parameters, return the computed result, and contain no side effects beyond construction. This enables table-driven unit testing without starting the manager. Functions are placed between init() and main() in cmd/main.go.
